title: Cuisinart DCG-20BKN Coffee Grinder1, Blade Grinder, BLACK
description:
About this item
EASILY GRIND LARGE QUANTITIES: You'll easily process up to 70g/2.5oz of beans at once; enough to make up to 12 cups of fresh coffee
MODERN DESIGN: We bring a modern touches to this coffee grinder with black body to seamlessly blend with your appliances
HEAVY-DUTY MOTOR: Stainless steel blades and high power motor with provides consistent results with on/off control for simple operation
LID FEATURES: We designed this coffee grinder to have a built-in safety interlock to prevent operation unless lid is securely closed
EXTRA PERKS: Easy-to-clean bowl and lid and convenient cord storage for ease of use; This product is BPA free and comes with an instruction book to assist with the set up and learning process
reviews_5s:
We were looking for a coffee grinder and came across Cuisinart. What caught our attention was a brand name as well as price. Gave it a try and have no complains. It's easy to use, it's quick ,it's not too noisy and it does the job well.
Shipping was super fast- we have received it overnight.Easy to clean.Holds enough for a standard pot of coffee. Grinds in about 10 to 20 seconds. Didn't know coffee grinders ground so quick as my food processor definitely doesn't (blade difference?). Not really concerned about cleaning it, but when I do will require a small paint brush or a damp paper towel. Anyways, a good purchase for me and recommended.Very efficient, grinds full load into fine powder within 1 min. A bit noisy, which it's kind of expected from this type of device, especially at such a low price. It doesn't have a on/off switch (which I guess is intentional, for safety reasons - to avoid overheating), but there are some smart ways to hack it.
My previous one was of the same brand and served for good 15 years; this one feels like somewhat more powerful. Hopefully will serve for a long term too.Works great and grinds it fine enough.Grinding your own beans is always the best choice, and I absolutely love this product!Cuisinart is a terrific company and this grinder was reasonably priced. My counter space is minimal and it fits next to my coffeemaker without issues.Grinding coffee beans.Thank youThis is my go to Grindr for my own daily use and one that I will send to clients occasionally if they do not have a grinder
reviews_1s:
Very unsure what happened here. I used it two days in a row without issue. Did not move it from the same spot on my counter. I went to use it the third day and nothing. I have never had this happen before.

My old coffee grinder would sometimes get clogged where the pin on the lid connects to the body to activate the motor but that was not the case. It did not come in contact with any liquids, it is not an issue with the outlet, I did not drop it, I did not over fill it, so I am unsure. Very strange.

It was working fine so I threw out the packaging but I was still able to get a full refund. I thought Cuisinart was a reputable brand but apparently not. I purchased a new one and went with a different brand this time just in case.Arrived quickly, as expected. Opened it, registered it. Used it on day one. Louder than I had hoped, but whatever. Then on day two the engine started smoking. Apparently, if you tell Amazon that the product they just barely shipped. You is already broken. They want you to pay for the return shipping. I can imagine many other Amazon members who are aware of this may simply clean and package the machine right up and send it back broken asking for a regular refund-I surely could have as I had the box and manual sitting right next to it when it broke. In any case, rather than pay shipping to Amazon as a fee for purchasing the wrong product, I went through the manufacturer to resolve the problem. They were easy enough to talk to and work with, but now half a month later I still haven’t received my grinder. Just got an email that it’s on its way, so hopefully by next week. Thanks for the “one day free shipping”, Amazon." The cuisinart (?) coffee grinder I bought less than 3 months ago ago has already broken. I am very disappointed in the quality and the design (it’s terrible!!!) I wish they would just go back to the deep style. It doesn’t need any fancy designs in the lid. Who came up with this anyways? Not a fan. Amazon will not refund because it’s a few weeks past the return by date. It didn’t break until right after. So I’m here to say. Stay away from this coffee grinder. It’s a waste of money. And time!! BUYER BEWAREI am returning this prior to using it so I can't speak to the product itself. I opened the package and found remnants of coffee grounds in the bowl. Clearly this was a return and was not even cleaned out before reselling. I expect better than that, so I won't be asking for a replacement. Disappointed....ughIt was ok while it lasted. Nothing special. Lasted a few months.

We purchased this on August 15th, and it broke a few days ago. Yes it received daily use, but so has my previous no name grinder (which lasted for years).

Pathetic how such a highly rated item such as this can't even do what it was designed to.

The thing started smoking and smelling of burnt plastic. Yikes.Worked great 3 times. The 4th time the blades started moving very slowly. Cleaned it out and now it won’t turn on at all.I should have read the low score reviews, but I figured it being Cuisinart there should be no issue. Boy was I wrong, it makes a mess as the lid has an inner chamber and an outer rim. The ground coffee bean debris gets spread between these two sections of the lid. The lid does not go on easy as you have to align the one part which triggers the power button. The taste of my coffee is also more bitter, I did not have that issue with the Braun one this replaced. The Braun was easier to clean as well, I am considering returning this and just staying with the Braun one.My last coffee grinder lasted me somewhere around 20 years. This one lasted two months before the motor burned up. I wouldn't recommend this, even though I have always trusted Cuisinart appliances in the past.We had a Cuisinart grinder for over 15 years and thought we’d buy another when it finally gave out- but this one didn’t last 3 days. The blade broke.I just opened the box, wiped the unit and gave the first try and first scoop of coffee, it did a good job. In the second run it just stopped running and never turned on again and a weird chemical smell came from the device. I’m pretty sure it was some wiring or the engine itself just burn because of a scoop of coffee beans! As a person who likes cuisinart, I was quite disappointed and returned the unit. I ordered bigger one now which seems more reliable. I hope the brand doesn’t fail me again with the new one.
